title: ficus tinctoria var. parasitica
intro: Ficus tinctoria var. parasitica, also known as Dysoxylum binectarium or Dysoxylum malabaricum, is a plant species that belongs to the family Meliaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that are believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared in various herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat various health conditions.
